title: Chapter 1: Introduction to Programming & Python
description: Define fundamental concepts like 'program' and 'algorithm,' navigate the Google Colab environment, write a 'Hello, World!' program, and use variables to store basic data types such as integers, floats, and strings.

title: Chapter 2: Expressions, Operators, and User Input
description: Learn to construct expressions using mathematical and string operators, get input from a user, store it, and convert between different data types (casting).

title: Chapter 3: Control Flow: Conditional Logic
description: Write programs that make decisions using `if`, `elif`, and `else` statements. Utilize comparison operators to create Boolean expressions for controlling program flow.

title: Chapter 4: Data Structures I: Lists
description: Introduction to lists for storing collections of values. Learn to access, modify, add, and remove list elements using indices, slices, and common methods.

title: Chapter 5: Control Flow: Iteration
description: Master program repetition using `for` loops to iterate over sequences and `while` loops for indefinite iteration. Understand the concepts of loop variables and state.

title: Chapter 6: The Art of Problem-Solving & Debugging
description: Develop skills to translate a problem description into pseudocode, apply a systematic debugging process, and interpret Python's traceback messages to locate and fix errors.

title: Chapter 7: Functions: Creating Reusable Code
description: Learn to define and use functions that accept parameters and return values, understand the difference between local and global scope, and decompose complex problems into smaller, manageable functions.

title: Chapter 8: Midterm Review & Exam
description: A comprehensive review week to synthesize and apply all concepts covered in the first half of the course, culminating in the midterm exam.

title: Chapter 9: Data Structures II: Dictionaries
description: Explore dictionaries for modeling data with key-value pairs. Learn to add, access, and remove items, and understand the differences between lists and dictionaries.

title: Chapter 10: Advanced String Manipulation & File I/O
description: Master powerful string manipulation methods and learn to perform file input/output (I/O) by opening, reading from, and writing to plain text files.

title: Chapter 11: Modules & the Python Standard Library
description: Learn to use the `import` statement to access code from external modules and solve problems using powerful tools from the Python Standard Library like `math`, `random`, and `csv`.

title: Chapter 12: A Deeper Dive into Python's Objects
description: Understand that everything in Python is an object with methods. Learn to use `dir()` and `help()` to explore object capabilities and master advanced methods for built-in data types.

title: Chapter 13: Error Handling & Final Project Launch
description: Learn to handle potential runtime errors gracefully using `try` and `except` blocks. This week also marks the beginning of the design and implementation of the final project.

title: Chapter 14: Course Wrap-up & Final Review
description: A comprehensive review of all major course topics in preparation for the final exam. This week also includes a discussion on pathways for continued learning in programming.

title: Chapter 15: Final Exam Week
description: The culmination of the course with the final examination, covering all material from the semester with an emphasis on topics from the second half.
